title: Testing against podman-next breaks test_container_mounts
body:
This recent upstream breaking change impacted the tests https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25942.

Probably podman-py testing should reflect only podman release branches


Impacts:
https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/513
https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/544
https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/546
https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/540

And other test runs

comments.body:
> Probably podman-py testing should reflect only podman release branches

A better solution could be to duplicate the tests runs and run the test suite against distribution `podman` and `podman-next`. `podman-next` is used to test against features that are coming in the next release, so it's crucial to be able to test against upstream changes sometimes.
By making use of pytest [skipping](https://docs.pytest.org/en/7.1.x/how-to/skipping.html) we could run the scenarios for stable features and for future features when we have one (which would also be a considerably small run).
